description: Tablet has unusual chemical smell on opening the bottle. Patient stated the odor was not present in previous prescriptions of the same product.
Tablet discoloration — tablets appear yellowish instead of white. Multiple tablets affected in the same bottle.
Patient developed fever and rigors 20 minutes after IV administration of saline. Suspected particulate contamination. Attending physician reported adverse event.
Outer carton label states 40mg but tablets inside are embossed with 20mg. Patient noticed discrepancy at pharmacy.
Capsule content appears clumped — powder not free-flowing when capsule opened. Pediatric patient unable to take dose mixed in food.
Tablets appear to have uneven weight — some tablets visibly smaller than others in the same bottle. Hospital pharmacy flagged during incoming inspection.
Black particulate matter visible in heparin vial before administration. Nurse noticed and did not administer. ICU setting — patient was critically ill.
Tablet coating peeling off — multiple tablets in bottle show visible flaking of the outer film coat.
Patient information leaflet missing from medication pack. Carton sealed correctly but no PIL inside. Pharmacy noticed during dispensing.
White flocculent particles observed in insulin pen cartridge. Diabetic patient noticed cloudiness and did not administer. Requires urgent investigation due to patient safety risk.
